subject: Chemistry
chapter: The d and f Block Elements

concepts.title: Electronic Configuration of Lanthanides and Actinides
concepts.description: The electronic configuration of the 4f or 5f orbitals of the lanthanides and actinides is similar to that of the 6s and 4f or 5f orbitals of the preceding elements.

concepts.title: Similarities between Lanthanides and Actinides
concepts.description: Lanthanides and Actinides show similar chemical properties due to the similar electronic configurations and ionic radii.

concepts.title: Oxidation States of Lanthanides
concepts.description: Lanthanides exhibit a wide range of oxidation states, but the most common ones are +2, +3, and +4.

concepts.title: Mnemonics for Lanthanides and Actinides
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'Lanthanides and Actinides are Loyal to Atomic Numbers' to remember the correct order of these elements.

concepts.title: Confusing Lanthanides and Actinides
concepts.description: Do not confuse lanthanides with actinides, as they are two separate series of elements with different electronic configurations and properties.

concepts.title: Units for Atomic Radii
concepts.description: Remember that atomic radii of lanthanides and actinides decrease as you move down the series due to poor shielding of the nuclear charge by the 4f and 5f electrons.

concepts.title: Anomalous Electronic Configuration of Scandium and Lanthanum
concepts.description: [Ar] 3d^1 4s^2 and [Xe] 6s^2 5d^1 6p^1 are the anomalous electronic configurations of Scandium and Lanthanum respectively, which makes these elements exhibit similar chemical properties.
